item.title: China-Linked APT GopherWhisper Abuses Legitimate Services in Government Attacks
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
Dubbed GopherWhisper, the group relies on multiple Go-based backdoors alongside custom loaders and injectors.

The post China-Linked APT GopherWhisper Abuses Legitimate Services in Government Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Pre-Stuxnet Sabotage Malware ‘Fast16’ Linked to US-Iran Cyber Tensions
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
It targeted high-precision calculation software to tamper with results and packed a self-propagation mechanism.

The post Pre-Stuxnet Sabotage Malware ‘Fast16’ Linked to US-Iran Cyber Tensions appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: In Other News: Unauthorized Mythos Access, Plankey CISA Nomination Ends, New Display Security Device
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News
item.description:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Supreme Court hacker sentenced, Lovable exposed user data, Google expands enterprise security. 

The post In Other News: Unauthorized Mythos Access, Plankey CISA Nomination Ends, New Display Security Device appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Why Cybersecurity Must Rethink Defense in the Age of Autonomous Agents
item.dc:creator: Torsten George
item.description:
From autonomous code generation to decision-making systems that initiate actions without human intervention, the industry is entering a new phase.

The post Why Cybersecurity Must Rethink Defense in the Age of Autonomous Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Locked Shields 2026: 41 Nations Strengthen Cyber Resilience in World’s Biggest Exercise
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs
item.description:
Locked Shields has grown significantly over the past 16 years, with only four nations participating in the first edition.

The post Locked Shields 2026: 41 Nations Strengthen Cyber Resilience in World’s Biggest Exercise appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: US Federal Agency’s Cisco Firewall Infected With ‘Firestarter’ Backdoor
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
The malware provides remote access and control of infected devices and maintains post-patching persistence.

The post US Federal Agency’s Cisco Firewall Infected With ‘Firestarter’ Backdoor appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Trump Administration Vows Crackdown on Chinese Companies ‘Exploiting’ AI Models Made in US
item.dc:creator: Associated Press
item.description:
The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies’ exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models.

The post Trump Administration Vows Crackdown on Chinese Companies ‘Exploiting’ AI Models Made in US appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Vulnerabilities Patched in CrowdStrike, Tenable Products
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs
item.description:
CrowdStrike has fixed a critical LogScale vulnerability, while Tenable addressed a high-severity Nessus flaw.

The post Vulnerabilities Patched in CrowdStrike, Tenable Products appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Bitwarden NPM Package Hit in Supply Chain Attack
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
Tied to a fresh Checkmarx supply chain attack claimed by TeamPCP, the incident references the Shai-Hulud worm.

The post Bitwarden NPM Package Hit in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Copperhelm Raises $7 Million for Agentic Cloud Security Platform
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News
item.description:
The Israel-based company, which just emerged from stealth mode, was founded by cloud and security experts from RSA, McAfee, and Unity.

The post Copperhelm Raises $7 Million for Agentic Cloud Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.
